name: Eira Lizeth Corral Sepúlveda
bio: Eira Corral Sepúlveda is a Latina environmental policy professional and current MWRD Commissioner, first elected in 2020. She previously served as an environmental policy advisor and has deep ties to suburban Cook County environmental justice communities. She is a key voice for equity in infrastructure investment and climate resilience at MWRD.
prior_office: MWRD Commissioner (elected 2020–present). Environmental policy background; civic work in Hanover Park, Berwyn, and North Riverside areas.

jacket.source: Illinois State Board of Elections (not retrieved)
jacket.note: Campaign finance data not pulled; check ISBE for current cycle filings

key_votes.bill: Approved 125 green infrastructure projects through MWRD cost-sharing program since 2020 (9.9M gallon retention capacity, 2,400+ structures protected)
key_votes.summary: Approved 125 green infrastructure projects through MWRD cost-sharing program since 2020 (9.9M gallon retention capacity, 2,400+ structures protected)

key_votes.bill: Helped develop MWRD's first Environmental Justice Policy
key_votes.summary: Helped develop MWRD's first Environmental Justice Policy

key_votes.bill: Supported creation of Environmental Justice Division
key_votes.summary: Supported creation of Environmental Justice Division

key_votes.bill: Supported Stickney and Kirie WRP phosphorus removal system installations
key_votes.summary: Supported Stickney and Kirie WRP phosphorus removal system installations

key_votes.summary: Part of launching 2024 multi-year research project with Argonne National Lab, University of Chicago, and Current

policy_platform.position: Infrastructure investment resilient to climate change
Environmental justice — equitable inclusion of diverse communities in infrastructure spending
Expand green infrastructure; downspout disconnection education for property owners
Nutrient reduction strategy (phosphorus, nitrogen) at water reclamation plants
Community ambassador role — annual Monarch Fests and grassroots outreach

career_history.role: Environmental policy professional with prior advisory roles
Community organizing in suburban Cook County
